formula_reproduction.note: Reproduced bartworks Werkstoff.getDurability()/getToolSpeed()/getToolQuality() (Werkstoff.java:708-743) from declaration data (protons/mass via WERKSTOFF_PROTONS/WERKSTOFF_MASS or ELEMENT-table fallback, meltingPoint via WERKSTOFF_MELTING_POINT or canonical MELTING_POINT fallback to 1123, contentsCount via WERKSTOFF_TYPE+WERKSTOFF_CONTENTS per Werkstoff.getContents() at Werkstoff.java:526-542).
formula_reproduction.unresolved_reason: 257 compounds/mixtures whose legacy protons AND mass fields were both 0 with an empty WERKSTOFF_CONTENTS list (division by contentsCount=0); canonical TOOL_QUALITY already carries -1 for nearly all of them, matching the Java (byte) cast of the resulting NaN/Infinity -- i.e. canonical already holds the correct degenerate legacy runtime value, no further materialization needed.

section3_atomic_mass.ground_truth_architecture: Legacy Materials.getProtons/getNeutrons/getMass (Materials.java:1855-1886): ELEMENT-backed -> Element enum literal (ground truth, Element.java); COMPOSITION-backed -> TWO DIFFERENT unreconciled formulas exist today: gregtech.api.enums.Materials uses a density-weighted average (density*sum(amount*sub.getX())/(totalAmount*M)), while gtPlusPlus.core.material.Material.calculateProtons/Neutrons uses a plain unweighted arithmetic mean. Neither is implemented against the new GTMaterialProperties.COMPOSITION property. This divergence is itself an unification decision, not merely a value pick.

GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.TOXIC: NEW canonical GTMaterialFlag/boolean (consumed live by WerkstoffHazardHandler.java:38-48 for poison potion effect off GTMaterialProperties directly, not just facade)
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.RADIOACTIVE: COLLAPSE onto a canonical RADIATION_LEVEL/IS_RADIOACTIVE property (also consumed live by WerkstoffHazardHandler for IC2 radiation potion effect)
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.LOCALIZED_FORMULA: KEEP as part of FORMULA/FORMULA_LOCALIZED unification (consumed by MaterialFormulas.java for tooltip rendering)
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.BLAST_FURNACE: COLLAPSE onto canonical BLAST_REQUIRED -- 31 materials co-present, 30 agree, 1 conflict (Alumina: flag=false, BLAST_REQUIRED=true)
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.ELECTROLYSIS: DELETE (facade-only; drives legacy WerkstoffReconstruction/GenerationFeatures recipe-gen, no direct GTMaterialProperties consumer found) -- or promote to NEW canonical HAS_ELECTROLYZER_RECIPE-style flag if recipe-gen is reimplemented against the new system; recommend NEW canonical since ELECTROLYSIS recipe generation is live gameplay, just currently reached only via the facade
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.CENTRIFUGE: same treatment as ELECTROLYSIS -- facade-only today, recommend NEW canonical if centrifuge auto-recipe-gen is reimplemented, else DELETE
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.METAL_CRAFTING_SOLIDIFICATION: DELETE or NEW canonical depending on whether metal-crafting-solidification recipe-gen is reimplemented; facade-only today
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.MIXING: COLLAPSE/NEW canonical alongside WERKSTOFF_MIX_CIRCUIT (see below) -- both gate the same Mixer auto-recipe
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.SIFTING: DELETE or NEW canonical; facade-only, only 3 materials
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.GAS: likely redundant with WERKSTOFF_TYPE=GAS / HAS_CORRESPONDING_GAS; DELETE
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.ENFORCE_UNIFICATION: DELETE or COLLAPSE onto canonical UNIFIABLE property (GTMaterialProperties.UNIFIABLE already exists) -- verify semantics match before collapsing
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.CHEMICAL_SYNTHESIS: DELETE or NEW canonical; facade-only, only 3 materials
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.NO_AUTO_BLAST_FURNACE_RECIPES: COLLAPSE onto canonical AUTO_BLAST_FURNACE_RECIPES (inverse boolean, already exists as its own property, 120 materials)
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.NO_AUTO_VACUUM_FREEZER_RECIPES: COLLAPSE onto canonical AUTO_VACUUM_FREEZER_RECIPES (inverse boolean, already exists, 78 materials) -- only 1 material carries this flag
GTWerkstoffFlag_member_fates.SUBLIMATION: DELETE or NEW canonical; facade-only, only 1 material

WERKSTOFF_MIX_CIRCUIT.reason: Live consumer: WerkstoffReconstruction.java feeds it into Werkstoff.GenerationFeatures.addMixerRecipes((short) mixCircuit), which configures the auto-generated Mixer recipe programmed-circuit number -- real recipe-gen behavior, not just facade bookkeeping, currently only reached via the legacy facade.

WERKSTOFF_EBF_GAS_TIME_MULTIPLIER_AND_AM.reason: Consumed by gregtech.api.util.BlastFurnaceGasStat / GTRecipeConstants.java (EBF recipe generation for blast-furnace gases) -- live recipe-duration/consumed-amount scaling.

GTPP_IS_RADIOACTIVE_RADIATION_LEVEL.fate: COLLAPSE onto GTWerkstoffFlag.RADIOACTIVE / a canonical RADIATION_LEVEL (unify the two radioactivity systems into one canonical pair)
GTPP_IS_RADIOACTIVE_RADIATION_LEVEL.reason: Currently reaches live gameplay (radiation damage, hazmat gating, recipe availability) only through the reconstructed legacy Material facade (MaterialReconstruction.java -> EntityUtils/GTUtility.applyRadioactivity); bartworks TOXIC/RADIOACTIVE reach WerkstoffHazardHandler directly. Post-unification these should be one property pair read directly by whatever hazard-handling code survives.

GTPP_USES_BLAST_FURNACE.reason: Present only when it diverges from BLAST_REQUIRED or BLAST_REQUIRED is absent.

GTPP_GENERATES_FLUID_CELLS.reason: Canonical/live fluid+cell generation is driven by HAS_CORRESPONDING_FLUID/HAS_CORRESPONDING_GAS/LEGACY_FLUIDS via LegacyMaterials.java and Materials2FluidShapes.java; GTPP_GENERATES_FLUID/CELLS only feed the legacy gtpp Material facade and dump tooling.

GTPP_TIER_VOLTAGE_MULTIPLIER.fate: NEW canonical TIER / VOLTAGE_MULTIPLIER properties (no gregtech equivalent exists)
GTPP_TIER_VOLTAGE_MULTIPLIER.reason: Pervasively read live (via the legacy Material facade today) across gtPlusPlus RecipeGen* codegen (plates, recycling, shaped crafting, conduits) and Forestry bee-drop classes to set recipe EU cost/tier -- dominant live driver of gtpp-derived recipe EU/tier.

GTPP_PLASMA_NAME.fate: KEEP as-is (already a well-scoped disambiguation pin), or fold into LEGACY_FLUIDS resolution logic
GTPP_PLASMA_NAME.reason: Only read by MaterialReconstruction.java to disambiguate a merged plasma fluid slot; no other live consumer found.

WERKSTOFF_SUB_TAGS.reason: Feeds legacy Werkstoff.SUBTAGS, consulted broadly by bartworks recipe-gen/tooltip logic via Werkstoff.contains(SubTag) (gas-type gating, no-blast gating, etc.) -- currently only reachable through the facade.

WERKSTOFF_CONTENTS_vs_COMPOSITION.what_changes: Salt/Spodumene/RockSalt currently carry a self-referencing single-entry WERKSTOFF_CONTENTS stub (e.g. Salt -> [(Salt,1)]) instead of real chemistry; COMPOSITION already has the correct breakdown (e.g. Salt -> Chlorine+Sodium). Electrolysis/centrifuge/mixer recipes generated off CONTENTS for these 3 materials are currently wrong/degenerate and must switch to COMPOSITION. SodiumNitrate (4th co-present material) already matches structurally, no change.

WERKSTOFF_ORE_BYPRODUCTS_vs_ORE_BYPRODUC.winner: ORE_BYPRODUCTS (canonical) for the 19 self-padding cases; case-by-case for the 3 real-structure cases
WERKSTOFF_ORE_BYPRODUCTS_vs_ORE_BYPRODUC.what_changes: 19 of 22: WERKSTOFF_ORE_BYPRODUCTS is padded with duplicate self-references (bartworks constructor behavior) while ORE_BYPRODUCTS already includes the material itself once plus real byproducts -- canonical wins, no real drop-table change. 3 of 22 (Spodumene, RockSalt real-structure diffs, Calcium/Tellurium/Alumina triple-self-pad vs single) need per-material review: Spodumene canonical byproducts (Alumina, Lithium) vs werkstoff (Spodumene self); RockSalt canonical (Borax, Salt) vs werkstoff (Borax, RockSalt self) -- canonical is the real drop table, werkstoff self-references are stale.

FORMULA_unification.winner: WERKSTOFF_FORMULA where WERKSTOFF_IDS present (matches current MaterialFormulas.java priority order); else FORMULA; else GTPP_CHEMICAL_FORMULA
FORMULA_unification.localization_key_split_note: MaterialFormulas.java keeps two separate localization-key builders: materialsFormulaKey (legacy Materials#getInternalName / LEGACY_NAME, used only with FORMULA_LOCALIZED) and werkstoffFormulaKey (legacy Werkstoff#getVarName / LOCAL_NAME, used only with GTWerkstoffFlag.LOCALIZED_FORMULA). Collapsing to one FORMULA property means collapsing to ONE key-building function too -- decide whether the surviving key uses LEGACY_NAME or LOCAL_NAME as the localization-key basis, since they differ for materials whose MaterialLib registration name was sanitized (LEGACY_NAME) vs simply de-spaced (LOCAL_NAME/werkstoffFormulaKey). Recommend keeping materialsFormulaKey's LEGACY_NAME-based logic as the single canonical key builder since FORMULA is the surviving property, and re-pointing any GTWerkstoffFlag.LOCALIZED_FORMULA-flagged materials at that same key scheme (verify none actually differ in practice; not checked in this pass).

new_canonical_properties: MIX_CIRCUIT (int, from WERKSTOFF_MIX_CIRCUIT, alongside a MIXING-equivalent flag)
TIER (int, from GTPP_TIER, no gregtech equivalent)
VOLTAGE_MULTIPLIER (long, from GTPP_VOLTAGE_MULTIPLIER, no gregtech equivalent)

DEFERRED/CONDITIONAL: ELECTROLYSIS_RECIPE, CENTRIFUGE_RECIPE, METAL_SOLIDIFICATION_RECIPE, SIFTING_RECIPE, CHEMICAL_SYNTHESIS_RECIPE flags -- only if the corresponding bartworks auto-recipe-gen is reimplemented against GTMaterialProperties directly; otherwise DELETE with the facade
deletion_list: WERKSTOFF_IDS, WERKSTOFF_POOL, WERKSTOFF_TYPE (facade identity/bookkeeping only, once reconstruction is removed)
GTPP_STATE (pure "has gtpp data" presence signal, dies with the facade)
GTPP_GENERATES_FLUID, GTPP_GENERATES_CELLS (superseded by HAS_CORRESPONDING_FLUID/HAS_CORRESPONDING_GAS/LEGACY_FLUIDS, which already drive live fluid/cell generation)

WERKSTOFF_DURABILITY_MODIFIER, WERKSTOFF_MELTING_VOLTAGE, WERKSTOFF_PREFIXES, WERKSTOFF_ORE_BYPRODUCTS, WERKSTOFF_CONTENTS, WERKSTOFF_PROTONS, WERKSTOFF_MASS, WERKSTOFF_FORMULA, WERKSTOFF_DURABILITY_OVERRIDE, WERKSTOFF_SPEED_OVERRIDE, WERKSTOFF_QUALITY_OVERRIDE (all materialized onto canonical DURABILITY/TOOL_SPEED/TOOL_QUALITY/ORE_BYPRODUCTS/COMPOSITION/FORMULA per sections 1-4 above, then deleted)

GTPP_CHEMICAL_FORMULA, GTPP_PROTONS, GTPP_NEUTRONS, GTPP_DURABILITY, GTPP_MELTING_POINT_K, GTPP_TIER (renamed not deleted), GTPP_VOLTAGE_MULTIPLIER (renamed not deleted), GTPP_USES_BLAST_FURNACE, GTPP_COMPOSITION, GTPP_PLASMA_NAME (all materialized/collapsed per sections 1-4, then deleted as raw GTPP_* keys)
unresolved_fields: COMPOSITION-backed proton/neutron/mass ground truth: two incompatible legacy formulas exist (density-weighted in gregtech.api.enums.Materials vs unweighted-mean in gtPlusPlus.core.material.Material) and neither is implemented against GTMaterialProperties.COMPOSITION today; picking one is a prerequisite for resolving WERKSTOFF_PROTONS vs GTPP_PROTONS divergence on SodiumNitrate and for the 122+234 composition-only materials pinning protons without any independently-computable ground truth.

4 melting-point divergences that are neither +273 conversions nor placeholder-beats (Dysprosium, Zirconium, Ruthenium, Rhodium) -- small (2-84K) unexplained gaps between canonical and gtpp; recommend spot-checking against a periodic-table reference before committing to "canonical wins".

Tool-stat formula reproduction left 4 small residual mismatches (Zirconium, Hafnium x2, Tellurium) whose exact cause (float truncation boundary vs a declaration-order subtlety) was not isolated within this pass.
WERKSTOFF_PREFIXES (391 materials) fate not fully researched -- likely overlaps ADDED_PREFIXES/REMOVED_PREFIXES but the exact relationship was not verified against a live consumer.

formula_gtpp.legacy: ⌘☯𓍰 𓍱 𓍲 𓍳 𓍴 𓍵 𓍶 𓍷 𓍸 ☯⌘
